subject: Project Management
title: Taiichi Ohno's Revolution
description: A quick, practical guide to Taiichi Ohno's Revolution.

hook.text: In the 1950s, Toyota was a tiny carmaker. Taiichi Ohno turned it into a global giant.

buildup.text: Ohno was obsessed with waste. Overproduction, waiting, excess inventory — all enemies.

discovery.text: He created the Toyota Production System — a philosophy of making only what's needed, when needed.

twist.text: Western factories mocked Toyota's small batches. Decades later, they scrambled to copy them.

climax.text: Ohno's system made Toyota the world's most efficient automaker — and inspired an entire movement.

punchline.text: The man who hated waste built the world's leanest company.

quiz.question: What was Taiichi Ohno's primary obsession?
